summary: iOS Hot Restart fails with type resolution error for 'SkiaSharp.GRBackendRenderTargetDesc' from SkiaSharp 2.88.0.0 when using SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui 2.0.0; upgrading to SkiaSharp 3.0-preview.13 resolves the issue.

bugSignals.errorMessage: Could not resolve type with token 0100002b from typeref (expected class 'SkiaSharp.GRBackendRenderTargetDesc' in assembly 'SkiaSharp, Version=2.88.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=0738eb9f132ed756')

stepsToReproduce: Create a .NET MAUI app referencing SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui 2.0.0
Deploy to iOS device using Hot Restart from Windows 11
Observe runtime type resolution failure on startup
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: .NET MAUI 9.0.30 SR3, SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui 2.0.0, Windows 11, Hot Restart to iOS

repoLinks.description: Original issue in dotnet/maui repo — transferred here

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: GRBackendRenderTargetDesc does not exist in the current SkiaSharp 3.x binding; the type was removed when deprecated SKSurface.Create overloads were cleaned up in v3.0.0.

regression.reason: Reporter states 'this used to work in .NET MAUI'. The error references SkiaSharp 2.88.0.0, suggesting a version mismatch between SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui 2.0.0 (compiled against SkiaSharp 2.88) and the loaded SkiaSharp assembly.

fixStatus.reason: Reporter confirms upgrading to SkiaSharp 3.0.0-preview.13 resolves the issue, most likely because SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui was updated to target SkiaSharp 3.x which removed the problematic GRBackendRenderTargetDesc references.

analysis.summary: Hot Restart on iOS fails because SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui 2.0.0 (compiled against SkiaSharp 2.88) references the type GRBackendRenderTargetDesc. Xamarin.iOS.HotRestart.ForceLoadingAssemblies scans all types and tries to resolve their constructor parameters via reflection; it cannot resolve GRBackendRenderTargetDesc, causing a fatal exception. GRBackendRenderTargetDesc was removed from the SkiaSharp codebase in v3.x (confirmed: no such class exists in the current binding/ directory), so the fix is to use an updated SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui targeting SkiaSharp 3.x.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug because this is a runtime crash that blocks the developer's debug workflow (Hot Restart). Classified area/SkiaSharp because the missing type (GRBackendRenderTargetDesc) is a SkiaSharp type. The root cause is an ABI incompatibility: SkiaSharp removed GRBackendRenderTargetDesc in v3.x while SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui 2.0.0 still depends on it. Suggested action is needs-info because no minimal repro was provided and the exact SkiaSharp NuGet version used in the failing scenario is unclear.

codeInvestigation.finding: GRBackendRenderTargetDesc is not referenced anywhere in this file. The type does not exist in the current SkiaSharp binding code, confirming it was removed in v3.x.
SKSurface.Create(GRContext, GRBackendRenderTargetDesc) overloads were removed as obsolete in v3.0.0. This confirms GRBackendRenderTargetDesc was present in v2.88 and removed in v3.0.

GRBackendRenderTarget(GRBackend, GRBackendRenderTargetDesc) was listed as a new constructor in v3.0.0 changelog, suggesting GRBackendRenderTargetDesc type existed in v2.88 and persisted at least briefly in v3.0 transition.

keySignals.text: Could not resolve type with token 0100002b from typeref (expected class 'SkiaSharp.GRBackendRenderTargetDesc' in assembly 'SkiaSharp, Version=2.88.0.0')
keySignals.interpretation: The Hot Restart assembly scanner encounters a type reference to GRBackendRenderTargetDesc from a SkiaSharp 2.88 assembly and cannot resolve it at runtime.
The crash is in Hot Restart's assembly pre-loading step which scans all types and their constructors via reflection.

keySignals.text: upgrading to a preview version 3.0.0.0-preview.13 seems to fix it
keySignals.interpretation: The SkiaSharp 3.0 preview either updated the Extended.UI.Maui library to not reference GRBackendRenderTargetDesc, or the v3 assembly handles type scanning differently.
Third-party extension library compiled against SkiaSharp 2.88 that contains GRBackendRenderTargetDesc references; updating this library is the likely fix.

nextQuestions: What exact SkiaSharp NuGet version is specified in the .csproj (not just the assembly version reported in the error)?
Does upgrading SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui to the latest 3.x version while keeping SkiaSharp 3.x stable resolve the issue permanently?
Does the error occur with a plain SkiaSharp MAUI app (no Extended.UI.Maui) via Hot Restart?
workarounds: Upgrade SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui to a version that targets SkiaSharp 3.x (reporter confirms 3.0.0-preview.13 works)
Use regular device deployment instead of Hot Restart for debugging

resolution.hypothesis: SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui 2.0.0 contains a type that has a constructor or method signature referencing GRBackendRenderTargetDesc (removed in SkiaSharp v3.x). Xamarin.iOS.HotRestart.ForceLoadingAssemblies scans all constructor signatures via reflection and fails when it can't resolve this type from the loaded SkiaSharp assembly.

proposals.title: Upgrade SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui to 3.x
proposals.description: Replace SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui 2.0.0 with the latest version targeting SkiaSharp 3.x. The reporter confirms SkiaSharp 3.0.0-preview.13 resolves it.

proposals.title: Use regular deploy instead of Hot Restart
proposals.description: Disable Hot Restart and use standard iOS device deployment for debugging. This avoids the ForceLoadingAssemblies scanning step.

resolution.recommendedReason: Directly addresses the root cause — the outdated library referencing removed SkiaSharp types. Reporter has confirmed it works.

actionability.reason: No minimal repro project available. Root cause points to SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui 2.0.0 using the removed GRBackendRenderTargetDesc type, but exact SkiaSharp NuGet version in the failing scenario is unspecified. Need to confirm if this is reproduced with a plain SkiaSharp MAUI app or only with the Extended library.

missingInfo: Exact SkiaSharp NuGet package version referenced in the project (.csproj)
Does the issue reproduce with a plain SkiaSharp MAUI app (without SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui)?
Minimal reproduction project showing the failure

actions.description: Apply bug, core SkiaSharp, iOS, compatibility, and MAUI partner labels
Request repro info and clarify SkiaSharp version; provide workarounds
actions.comment:
Thanks for the report and for providing the stack trace!

Based on the error, `Xamarin.iOS.HotRestart.Application.ForceLoadingAssemblies` is trying to scan constructor signatures via reflection and encountering a reference to `SkiaSharp.GRBackendRenderTargetDesc` — a type that was removed from SkiaSharp in v3.x.

This appears to originate from `SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui 2.0.0`, which was compiled against SkiaSharp 2.88.x and still references that removed type. Upgrading to a version of `SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui` that targets SkiaSharp 3.x should resolve the issue (which aligns with your observation that the 3.0.0-preview.13 package fixes it).

**Workarounds while waiting for a stable release:**
1. Upgrade `SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui` to the latest 3.x preview version
2. Use standard device deployment instead of Hot Restart for debugging

To help us investigate further, could you provide:
- The exact `SkiaSharp` NuGet version in your `.csproj`
- Does the issue reproduce without `SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui` (plain SkiaSharp only)?
